symptoms.symptom_name: Reported single pharmacy blood pressure of 180/100 mmHg while anxious

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Isolated high reading in a high-arousal clinical/machine setting is the hallmark trigger; patient-reported and unverified, so weight is moderate.
A high clinic reading is consistent with but not diagnostic of sustained hypertension without out-of-office confirmation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Completely asymptomatic — no headache, chest pain, breathlessness or visual change

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of any target-organ symptom makes an acute hypertensive emergency very unlikely.
An asymptomatic high reading fits a situational (white-coat) pattern.

symptoms.symptom_name: No acute stroke features (no focal weakness, facial droop, dysarthria or thunderclap headache)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Negative cerebrovascular red-flag screen argues strongly against an emergency requiring 000.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prior high readings only in clinical/machine settings, never symptomatic

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A consistent pattern of setting-specific elevation is characteristic of the white-coat effect.

symptoms.symptom_name: Marked situational anxiety around healthcare settings and equipment

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Situational anxiety is the mechanistic driver of the white-coat sympathetic surge.
The anxiety is real and is a comorbid contributor, though strictly situational rather than a generalised panic disorder.

symptoms.symptom_name: Resting blood pressure normalised to 125/80 mmHg after 15 minutes' quiet rest at home

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Complete normalisation of pressure once the clinical trigger is removed is the defining feature of white-coat hypertension. Sealed: a clinician measurement the AI Doctor cannot obtain remotely.
A normal resting pressure is incompatible with an active hypertensive emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal fundoscopy — no retinal arteriolar narrowing, silver-wiring or haemorrhages

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of hypertensive retinopathy argues against chronic sustained hypertension with target-organ damage. Sealed: requires clinician fundoscopy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal cardiac exam — dual heart sounds, no murmur, no left ventricular heave

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No left ventricular hypertrophy on palpation argues against long-standing sustained hypertension. Sealed: requires clinician examination.

investigation_details.expected_result: Anticipated normal out-of-office average, confirming white-coat hypertension
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without ABPM or a home BP diary, white-coat and sustained (or masked) hypertension cannot be reliably separated.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: ABPM is the reference standard that confirms normal out-of-office pressure and secures the diagnosis.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Elevated clinic reading + asymptomatic + setting-specific history + normal resting pressure together define the white-coat pattern.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The AI cannot measure BP remotely; a calm, correctly-measured in-person reading today confirms whether the pharmacy value reflects true pressure.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home blood-pressure monitoring with a validated cuff (diary)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Standardised weekly home readings after 10 minutes' rest provide out-of-office data to distinguish white-coat from sustained hypertension.
Reference-standard confirmation of out-of-office blood pressure; arranged via GP follow-up.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate ED transfer / hypertensive-emergency workup (bloods, ECG, imaging)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated in a completely asymptomatic patient with a negative target-organ red-flag screen — this would be over-investigation driven by the alarming number.
